__fixture__.name: entry_row_deep_link_intent_review
__fixture__.scenario: An aureline:// deep link from a notification arrives requesting reattach of a managed-cloud workspace. The handler does not call a private opener; it routes through the entry chooser. The chooser computes the verb against the actual target (managed-cloud always resumes; never opens) and emits one entry_chooser_row_record with entry_chooser_row_kind = deep_link_intent_review, entry_chooser_surface_class = deep_link_intent_review, source_surface = deep_link, entry_verb_candidate = resume, trust_posture_class = trust_revalidated_at_resume. The proposed link verb is never trusted; the resolver computes the final verb. Activation pairs with the deep_link_intent_review sheet which discloses origin, handler ownership, replay posture, and authority delta before any commit. Demonstrates §6 invariant 4 (deep links route through the chooser) and the workspace-entry route matrix rule that a deep link's own claim of authority is never trusted.
contract_sections: §3 The project-entry verb matrix (open row, deep-link source)
§6 rule 4 Deep links route through the chooser

presentation_subtitle: Origin and handler verified. Authority widening requires review before open.
